data.title: Pfizer gets FDA accelerated approval for Braftovi
data.description:
Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has received FDA accelerated approval for its drug Braftovi in combination with other therapies as a first-line treatment for metastatic colorectal cancer, or mCRC, with a BRAF gene mutation.

The agency approved the medication, also known as encorafenib, to be used in combination with cetuximab, also known as Erbitux, and mFOLFOX6 chemotherapy for the treatment of patients with mCRC with a BRAF V600E mutation, as detected by an FDA-approved test, according to a statement.

Pfizer said that continued approval of the product for this indication is contingent upon verification of clinical benefit.

Braftovi was previously approved for use in combination with cetuximab for adults with mCRC with a BRAF V600E mutation after prior therapy.

Erbitux is marketed by Eli Lilly (LLY).

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: RGTI, CCL, NVO, DXCM and more
data.description:
U.S. stocks on Friday jumped, after investors received a favorable reading on a key inflation measure that further quelled the turmoil induced by the Federal Reserve two days ago.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Rigetti Computing (RGTI) stock surged more than 25% in afternoon trade, as quantum stocks continued to remain in the spotlight following Alphabet's (GOOG)(GOOGL) unveiling of its "mind-boggling" Willow quantum chip earlier this month. Craig-Hallum started coverage on the stock with a Buy rating and a $12 price target.
  • Class A shares of Xometry (XMTR) advanced more than 13%, after Wedbush initiated coverage on the stock with an Outperform rating. The brokerage sees the firm to be well-positioned to take share within the global custom manufacturing industry. North Bethesda, Md.-based Xometry (XMTR) runs an AI-powered marketplace for industrial parts.
  • Diabetes stocks saw some gains in afternoon trade. Dexcom (DXCM) added 6.2%, Tandem Diabetes Care (TNDM) rose 7.6%, and Insulet (PODD) swelled 4.2%. The moves came after Novo Nordisk (NVO) announced disappointing results from a late-stage trial of its next-gen weight loss therapy, CagriSema.
  • Shares of Carnival (CCL) improved more than 6%, after the world's largest cruise line operator delivered a top- and bottom-line FQ4 2024 beat. It highlighted strong booking volumes for the quarter, despite a "traditionally slower period around the election." Additionally, the company forecasted adjusted net income of about $2.3B for full year 2025, representing growth of more than 20% than 2024.
  • Mission Produce (AVO) shares jumped about 19% after FQ4 results exceeded expectations, driven by a 37.4% rise in sales and a 160% increase in adjusted profits, fueled by higher avocado prices. For FQ1, the company expects stable industry avocado volumes but anticipates a 20% rise in pricing, signaling continued strong demand.

Biggest stock losers

  • Novo Nordisk (NVO) U.S.-listed shares plummeted nearly 17% on Friday, while its rival Eli Lilly (LLY) surged 3%, after the Danish drugmaker released late-stage trial data for its next-generation weight loss therapy, CagriSema, which fell short of expectations. The once-weekly injectable demonstrated up to ~23% weight loss, outperforming its FDA-approved obesity treatment, semaglutide, which achieved ~16% weight loss in the REDEFINE 1 Phase 3 trial. The company highlighted that CagriSema, a combination of amylin-analog cagrilintide and GLP-1 receptor agonist semaglutide, was safe and well-tolerated over 68 weeks. Other trial results showed ~2% weight loss for placebo, while ~40% of patients on CagriSema and ~16% on semaglutide achieved 25% or more weight loss.
  • United States Steel (X) shares dropped more than 5% after the company lowered its Q4 guidance, citing weak demand and pricing in Europe. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter is now expected to be around $150M, down from the prior forecast of $225M to $275M, while adjusted net earnings per diluted share are projected between -$0.29 and -$0.25, much below the consensus of $0.28. CEO David Burritt highlighted continued pressure from low steel prices and ramp-related costs at Big River Steel, alongside reduced selling prices, volumes, and increased maintenance in the Flat-Rolled segment. The company also expects weaker adjusted EBITDA in its Mini Mill and European segments due to lower volumes.
  • Nike (NKE) shares fell about 1% despite exceeding estimates in fiscal Q2, as the sportswear giant outlined its turnaround plan under new CEO Elliott Hill. The company is taking bold steps to revive the brand, including aggressively clearing aged inventory, strengthening its digital presence, and curbing promotional pricing. However, these initiatives will take time and require significant investment, with Nike warning of headwinds to its financial performance for the remainder of the year. The company projects Q3 revenue to decline by a low double-digit percentage, falling short of analyst expectations for an 8% drop, alongside a 300-350 basis point contraction in gross margin. Additionally, Nike expects an even steeper revenue decline in Q4 and anticipates its 2025 summer order books will be lower than last year. “This will take time,” Hill said during his first earnings call as CEO, “But I’m confident we are making the right move.”

More on related stocks:

data.title: First Quantum gains amid report CEO may seek Trump's help to reopen Panama mine
data.description:
First Quantum Minerals (OTCPK:FQVLF) rose 7.3% at least partly after a report that the mining company may seek help from President-elect Donald Trump to reopen the $10 billion Cobre Panama mine.

The mine has been shuttered since November 2023 due to disputes over tax rates and other issues.

“There’s a huge opportunity for the incoming administration in this space,” First Quantum CEO Tristan Pascall told Semafor in a story published on Friday. “There’s absolutely a deal for Trump to do on copper that lines up with the strategic interests of the US and of Panama.”

Bloomberg reported in late August that First Quantum Minerals (OTCPK:FQVLF) is seeking compensation from Panama for a stockpile of semi-processed copper ore stranded at the shuttered Cobre Panama mine as part of wider arbitration proceedings,

First Quantum (OTCPK:FQVLF) is seeking at least $20 billion from Panama in two arbitration cases filed after the government ordered the mine closed last November following public protests.

data.title: IO Biotech announces up to €57.5M debt financing from the European Investment Bank
data.description:
• IO Biotech (NASDAQ:IOBT) has entered into a loan facility of up to €57.5M from the European Investment Bank.
  • The debt facility includes three committed tranches totaling up to €37.5M, which will become available if the company satisfies certain conditions, and one uncommitted accordion tranche of €20M.
  • The company expects the first two tranches to be available for disbursement in Q1, 2025.
  • The company has 36 months to satisfy the conditions for the third tranche, which include raising an additional $50M in cash and submitting an application for marketing authorization for IO102-IO103 in the U.S. or the EU.
  • Funds are expected to be used to advance IO Biotech’s immune-modulating therapeutic cancer vaccines, including potential BLA submission for IO102-IO103 in 2025.
  • Debt facility is expected to extend company’s cash runway into Q2, 2026.
  • The company has agreed to issue to the European Investment Bank warrants to purchase a number of shares of the company’s common stock.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Samsung finalizes $4.75B CHIPs Act award
data.description:
The Biden administration finalized a $4.745B grant for South Korea's Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) as part of the $53B CHIPs Act on Friday.

The amount is roughly $1.7B smaller than previously announced in April, which the U.S. Commerce Department said was due to Samsung's revised investment plans. The money will be used for the development and production of leading-edge chips in Texas, including two new leading-edge logic fabs, an R&D fab in Taylor, and an expansion of Samsung's existing facility in Austin.

Samsung is one of the largest semiconductor foundries in the world, competing against companies like Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) and Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM).

“With this investment in Samsung, the U.S. is now officially the only country on the planet that is home to all five leading-edge semiconductor manufacturers,” said U.S. Secretary of Commerce Gina Raimondo in a statement.

“This is an extraordinary achievement, which will ensure we have a steady, domestic supply of the most advanced semiconductors that are essential to AI and national security, while also creating tens of thousands of good-paying jobs and transforming communities across the country.”

Separately, on Friday, the Commerce Department finalized its $1.6B grant for Texas Instruments (TXN).

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Labor pains for Amazon & Starbucks, Nasdaq call-up for MicroStrategy and Pony AI ratings
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Monday - December 23

  • Volatility watch - Galectin Therapeutics (GALT) and Neumora Therapeutics (NMRA) head the list of stocks showing high implied volatility based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Nukkleus Inc (NUKK), Quantum Computing (QUBT), and Quantum (QMCO). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Inovio (INO), Global Atomic (OTCQX:GLATF), and Nordson (NDSN). Short interest is elevated on Cassava Sciences (SAVA) and Hertz Global (HTZ).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Limoneira Company (LMNR).
  • Index shuffles: Palantir (PLTR), MicroStrategy (MSTR), and Axon Enterprise (AXON) will officially join the Nasdaq 100 Index to replace Super Micro Computer (SMCI), Moderna (MRNA), and Illumina (ILMN), as part of a reconstitution of the index. Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) and Workday (WDAY) will replace Qorvo (QRVO) and Amentum Holdings (AMTM) in the S&P 500 Index. Qorvo (QRVO) and Amentum Holdings (AMTM) will replace Kelly Services Inc. (KELYA) and Service Properties Trust (SVC) in the S&P SmallCap 600, respectively.
  • Labor check: Starbucks (SBUX) will be watched to see if a barista strike in three cities expands. Amazon (AMZN) is dealing with a Teamsters strike at seven warehouses that could also grow larger.
  • All day - The post-IPO quiet period expires on Pony AI (PONY) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • All day - The IPO lock-up periods expire on WEBTOON (WBTN), Alumis (ALMS), and LandBridge (LB).
  • All day - Covenant Logistics (CVLG) will begin to trade on a split-adjusted basis following the 2-for-1 stock split.
  • All day- It is the expiration date of the CFIUS review on the US Steel (X)-Nippon Steel deal.

Tuesday - December 24

  • All day - U.S. stock markets will close at 1:00 p.m. The bond markets will close at 2:00 p.m.
  • All day - Canoo (GOEV) will begin to trade on a split-adjusted basis following the 1-for-20 reverse stock split.
    • 8:30 a.m. The November Durable Orders report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 10:00 a.m. The New Home Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.

Wednesday - December 25

  • All day - The U.S. markets and most global markets will be closed for observance of the Christmas holiday.

Thursday - December 26

  • All day - Some global markets will be closed as the Boxing Day holiday is observed in the UK, Canada, Australia, and South Africa.
  • All day - The FDA action date hits for Rhythm Pharmaceuticals' (RYTM) Imcivree for obesity.
  • All day - CorVel Corporation (CRVL) is expected to start trading on a split-adjusted basis following the 3-for-1 stock split.
  • All day - It is the ex-dividend date for quarterly dividend payouts from Philip Morris International (PM) and Southwest Airlines (LUV).

Friday - December 27

  • Strategy watch - The Wall Street Gambit event over the weekend will combine the challenging arenas of chess and finance for a day of strategic thinking and competition. The event will feature some of the most renowned chess legends, including Magnus Carlsen, Fabiano Caruana, and Viswanathan Anand, who will be joined by leading figures from the financial and tech worlds, including hedge fund legend Boaz Weinstein, Kaggle CEO D. Sculley, and economist Kenneth Rogoff.
  • All day - The FDA action date hits for Soleno Therapeutics' (SLNO) Diazoxide Choline for Prader-Willi Syndrome.
  • All day - China-based water treatment provider Decent Holding (DXST) is expected to start trading after pricing its IPO.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: RBC downgrades Hookipa, slashes price target
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets has downgraded Hookipa Pharma (NASDAQ:HOOK) to sector perform from outperform, citing the company's restructuring plan, which includes eliminating 80% of its workforce and pausing development of its lead drug program.

RBC said it still liked the "promise" of Hookipa's programs and platform in oncology and infectious diseases and sees potential for partnerships and M&A deals.

"However, such timing is challenging to predict, and with the strategic refocus essentially now resetting the proprietary pipeline to preclinical stages - where it is difficult to ascribe meaningful value as of yet - we expect shares to trade near-term in the range of future cash levels," RBC added.

The investment firm slashed its price target for the stock to $2 from $48.

data.title: CFPB sues JPMorgan, BofA, Wells Fargo over fraud on Zelle (upated)
data.description:
Updated at 2:19 PM ET with explanation of $870M amount in third paragraph and Zelle comments in fifth, sixth and eighth paragraphs. Updates stock activity.

The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau sued the operator of Zelle and three of the country's largest banks — JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM), Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), and Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) — on Friday, alleging they allowed widespread fraud on the peer-to-peer payment platform.

Early Warning Service, which operates Zelle, and three of its owner banks "rushed the network to market to compete against growing payment apps such as Venmo and CashApp, without implementing effective consumer safeguards," the agency said on Friday.

The consumer financial services watchdog agency said that Zelle customers have lost more than $870M during its seven-year existence. That number, though, totals up fraud claims' reported losses, according to a person familiar with the matter.

The CFPB lawsuit said hundreds of thousands of consumers filed fraud complaints and were largely denied assistance, with some being told to contact the perpetrators of the fraud to recover their money. The three banks allegedly failed to properly investigate complaints or provide consumers with legally required reimbursement for fraud and errors, the CFPB said.

Zelle said that the firm reimburses customers for all instances of fraud as required by law under the Electronic Funds Transfer Act and Reg E.

"All claims of fraud or scams to a financial institution are required to be investigated, but that all claims may not be substantiated," a Zelle spokesperson said in an email to Seeking Alpha. "When a bank confirms that a consumer has been the victim of unauthorized activity on the Zelle Network, the consumer has zero liability for that activity and is fully reimbursed as mandated by network rules."

"By their failing to put in place proper safeguards, Zelle became a gold mine for fraudsters, while often leaving victims to fend for themselves," said CFPB Director Rohit Chopra.

The Zelle spokesperson countered: "We have made every effort to engage and cooperate with the CFPB on this matter; however, they fail to acknowledge Zelle is an essential part of protecting Americans from fraud and scams due to our highly effective multilayered fraud and scam prevention countermeasures. The Bureau also fails to acknowledge our consumer reimbursement policies that already go beyond legal and regulatory requirements."

Early Warning Services is co-owned by seven banks, including JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM), Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC), PNC Bank (PNC), Truist Financial (TFC), and U.S. Bancorp (USB).

The CFPB’s lawsuit seeks to halt unlawful conduct, obtain redress for harmed consumers, and obtain a civil money penalty, which would be paid into the CFPB’s victims relief fund, and secure other appropriate relief.

The news didn't appear to harm the banks' stock in afternoon trading. Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) stock rose 2.0%, JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) +2.2%, and Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) increased 2.6%.

data.title: Willis Towers Watson upgraded to Buy by Jefferies on sale of unit, efficiences in risk, brokering
Bitcoin to fall W/W as post-Fed dip extends, government shutdown looms
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell 4.3% on a weekly basis, as hawkish statements from the Federal Reserves fueled to a sell off.

The cryptocurrency started the week off quite strongly, as it touched a record high of ~$108K on Tuesday, however, its rise tapered as the Fed signalled fewer cuts in 2025 on Wednesday. BTC went below the $100K mark on Thursday, and at the time of writing has failed to go past it.

The Fed cut rates by 25 basis points, as expected, and signalled two more cuts next year. Fed Chair Powell also said that the Fed is "not allowed to hold Bitcoin (BTC-USD)", and it's not looking for a law change that would allow that.

“Bitcoin's price dip is likely more correlated to Fed Chair Jerome Powell's recent comments indicating fewer rate cuts in 2025 than it is related to the shutdown drama," said Cory Klippsten, CEO of Swan, a bitcoin financial services firm.

Crypto markets suffered extended losses as Trump's plans to create a U.S. Bitcoin strategic reserve may fall apart even before he gets in office after Powell said that the Fed is not allowed to hold Bitcoin and they're not looking for a law change, Nick Ruck, director at LVRG Research.

Wall Street's major market averages also witnessed a sell-off after the Fed's statements.

"Although an interest rate cut would normally have a bullish reaction since it was largely expected, the market strongly reacted after Fed Chair Jerome Powell stated that inflation would be a continuing problem throughout the next year," Ruck said.

The U.S.-listed spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds also saw record outflows on Thursday, a CoinDesk report stated.

"But in the mid to long run, we believe that monetary and fiscal stimulus policies in both the US and other parts of the world will ultimately expand liquidity across financial markets, especially as other countries seek to protect their economies against Trump tariffs. This will boost crypto markets, and especially Bitcoin as it becomes more of a safe haven asset akin to gold," said Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE.

Bitcoin's fall from the $100K mark comes amid fears of a government shutdown in the U.S., after a spending bill backed by President-elect Donald Trump was voted down in the U.S. House of Representatives.

Other Notable News

  • El Salvador will sell or discontinue its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) wallet Chivo as part of a new deal with the International Monetary Fund.
  • Block (SQ) was upgraded by Oppenheimer to Outperform from Perform as analyst Rayna Kumar laid out three key tailwinds.
  • MicroStrategy (MSTR) is poised to "go back and build more intelligent leverage for the benefit of our common stock shareholders" as it relates to the Bitcoin (BTC-USD) development company's capital structure, co-founder and Chairman Michael Saylor said.
  • MicroStrategy (MSTR) and Coinbase (COIN) are most likely to experience a short squeeze or a rally spurred by short sellers exiting their bearish bets
  • Riot Platforms (RIOT) acquired 667 Bitcoin (BTC-USD) for about $67.5M at average price of $101,135 per coin, it disclosed on Monday.

Bitcoin, Ether news

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) down 1.7% to ~$97.1K at 2:16 pm on Friday. Ether (ETH-USD) fell 1.7% to $3.4K.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

More on crypto
